danger_signals.visual_form: incoming shots from enemies on screen
danger_signals.meaning: immediate threat requiring evasion or blocking

danger_signals.visual_form: enemies appearing from stage edges or specific locations
danger_signals.meaning: upcoming combat encounter; escalating difficulty as more spawn

danger_signals.visual_form: environmental obstacles (mine cart walls, helicopter terrain, etc.)
danger_signals.meaning: stage-specific dangers that damage or disrupt player movement

danger_signals.visual_form: large-scale enemies with distinctive visual design
danger_signals.meaning: major threat requiring strategy; stage culmination encounter
accumulating damage; risk of death if not restored

safety_signals.meaning: reward and player empowerment; increased survivability

safety_signals.visual_form: item drops from defeated enemies (homing shot, lightning blaster, flamethrower, machine gun)
safety_signals.meaning: tactical advantage; ability to adapt loadout for upcoming threats

safety_signals.visual_form: reaching end of level; transition to next stage
safety_signals.meaning: progression forward; safety checkpoint in campaign

safety_signals.visual_form: menu UI allowing non-linear stage choice
safety_signals.meaning: player agency and strategic approach selection; can tackle easier stages first

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: dual character team identity (Gunstar Red, Gunstar Blue)
represented through enemy types and boss designs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: multiple enemy archetypes encountered across stages (ground troops, airborne units, specialized threats)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: stage themes reflect empire control (industrial, military, treasure/gem locations)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: four powerful gems are objective targets; stages represent empire locations holding gems
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player progression through stages indicates reclaiming each gem; stage completion = gem acquisition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: stage variety and enemy density communicate empire's resource allocation and defensive capabilities
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: escalating difficulty and boss encounters reflect empire's organized military hierarchy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: mine cart segments, helicopter combat, board game levels visually distinguish stage environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player must adapt combat strategy to stage-specific mechanics; demonstrates empire's diverse operational capacity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: vitality meter increases; weapon variety visible in HUD
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: players grow stronger through stage progression; stat increases enable tackling later, harder stages

environmental_storytelling.example: different environments (mine, helicopter, board game arena) tell story of empire's diverse holdings and operations without dialogue
enemy visual designs match stage themes; enemies appear faction-aligned to their deployment location
large-scale boss visuals signal major empire figures/assets; defeating bosses advances narrative of empire degradation
first four stages playable in any order suggest gem locations are independently held; final stages are linear, suggesting central empire stronghold approach

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: fire projectiles, spawn in coordinated patterns, demonstrate tactical awareness
large-scale attack patterns, phase transitions, unique movement mechanics distinguish them from minions
can choose free or fixed firing stance; acrobatic maneuvers (jump, slide, grab-throw) communicate high mobility and tactical flexibility
dual-character gameplay allows coordinated attacks, flanking, and shared threat distribution

notes: Gunstar Heroes uses visual and mechanical language to communicate empire threat magnitude through enemy density, boss design, and stage variety. The four-gem objective structure and stage format variation (mine cart, helicopter, board game) create tactical narrative without explicit storytelling. Player empowerment through vitality and weapon pickups is visually communicated via HUD and item drops. The cooperative two-character system (Red/Blue) is central to world-state communication—players experience dual-protagonist perspective throughout. Stage completion = gem reclamation in world-state terms. No explicit faction color-coding found in source material, but enemy/boss design clearly signals empire allegiance through contextual appearance.
